content.claim: Hungary has one of the strongest socioeconomic-status/performance gradients among OECD countries in PISA mathematics, reading, and science; however, most students are in their first year of secondary education at age 15 PISA testing, so performance differences cannot be fully attributed to track type.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Correlational/aggregate evidence does not prove causal link to 6-/8-year track structure
Performance gradient may reflect selection effects during primary schooling, within-school sorting, or pre-school/family factors
PISA testing at age 15 occurs after selection occurs (age 10–12), but not late enough to isolate track effect from other secondary-school factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
